Okay, here's my thought process on this one. I've got "strain" and "jerk" as my team words, and I need a single-word clue. My opponent's words are "pro, pound, fit, fleet, beetle", and I have a bunch of distractors and the kill word "ham" to consider.

"PULL" jumped out at me immediately because both "strain" (pull a muscle, physical pull) and "jerk" (a quick pull) work well with it. I did a quick check: "PULL" doesn't seem to connect with any other words, which is good.

"MUSCLE" was tempting because of "strain a muscle" and "muscle jerk," but "fit" could be misinterpreted as a direct link to "muscle" and I don't want to get mixed up with that. "SPRAIN" also came to mind, since "strain" and "sprain" are often linked, but "jerk" is a bit of a stretch there. "SUDDEN" isn't bad (jerk is sudden, strain can be sudden), but it feels a little too abstract. "TENSION" wasn't quite right for "jerk" either.

I even considered "WRENCH." "Wrench a muscle" to "strain" is solid, and "wrenching motion" for "jerk" works, but it might be too obscure. I kept coming back to "PULL". So I quickly went through the words again to ensure it wasn't a double meaning with them. Nothing.

"EXERT" could work, but it felt a little too indirect. "PHYSICAL" was way too broad. "TUG" is solid, but "PULL" is even more direct.

So, after all that analysis, I think the best option here is still **"PULL"**. It's the most direct and semantically strong link for both "strain" and "jerk," and it doesn't seem to have any risky connections to the other words in play.
